data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "No critic-filtering architecture can strictly beat self-consistency when the candidate pool and critic share the same model family, because the critic's error pattern is positively correlated with the candidate pool's error pattern (shared blind spots), making falseflag_rate_correct ≥ recall_wrong on the questions that matter. Harness strictly beats SC only with an orthogonal critic (zero error-correlation with candidates) in the mid-zone error regime.", "mechanism": "Self-consistency fails when the majority is wrong. A critic can only help by catching wrong candidates that the majority missed. But if the critic shares the model family's blind spots (same training data, same reasoning patterns), it will fail to catch exactly those wrong candidates that the pool also got wrong (low recall_wrong on the relevant errors) while still having nonzero falseflag rate on correct candidates (which the pool got right but the critic may second-guess). This creates a structural inequality: recall_wrong < falseflag_rate_correct whenever the shared-error correlation is positive. The harness can only strictly beat SC when recall_wrong > falseflag_rate_correct, which requires the critic's errors to be uncorrelated with (or anti-correlated with) the candidate pool's errors — an orthogonal critic. The observed data (v4-pro critic on v4-pro candidates, v4-flash critic on v4-flash candidates) exhibits exactly this failure: same-family critics with positive error correlation, yielding net zero or net harm.", "scope": {"covers": ["any k ≥ 5", "mid-zone e in (1/(k+1), k/(k+1))", "critic from a DIFFERENT model family than candidates (orthogonal error pattern)"], "excludes": ["same-family critic (structurally cannot strictly beat SC)", "low-zone or high-zone error (ties or losses regardless of critic orthogonality)"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "A same-family critic (e.g., v4-pro critic on v4-pro candidates) at k=7, e≈0.33 strictly beats self-consistency — if so, the orthogonality requirement is refuted", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "An orthogonal critic (e.g., Claude critic on Gemini candidates) at k=7, e≈0.33 does NOT strictly beat self-consistency despite recall_wrong > falseflag_rate_correct — if so, orthogonality is insufficient and the mechanism is incomplete", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Measured error correlation between same-family critic and candidate pool is ≤ 0 (non-positive), contradicting the shared-blind-spot assumption — if so, the causal story is wrong", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This is the atypical candidate: it identifies a confound (shared model family) that may explain ALL observed null results, and predicts that the strict-benefit regime requires crossing model families. The decisive experiment has 4 cells: {same-family, cross-family} × {fixed critic, adaptive critic}, all at k=7, e≈0.33 (mid-zone). Each cell: 120 items × 5 seeds = 600 samples. Cross-family cells use Gemini-2.0-flash as critic on Gemini-2.5-pro candidates and vice versa. Token budget: 4 cells × ~65k tokens ≈ 260k, within 300k. The critical prediction is that ONLY the cross-family cells show strict benefit, while same-family cells tie or lose regardless of adaptivity."}
